scenario_title: Same-name add and delete_rrset in one UPDATE
test_rationale: This scenario isolates the underspecified case of a single dynamic update message that both adds an RR and deletes the containing RRset for the same owner and type. The prerequisite makes the RRset present so the delete_rrset is meaningful, while the follow-up query distinguishes implementations that accept the message but end with an empty RRset from those that reject or preserve the preexisting data. Because RFC 2136 does not define a required intra-message ordering or atomic conflict policy for same-name add/delete_rrset combinations, BIND and Knot can legitimately diverge here.

group_1.behavior: Applies the add and then the delete_rrset, or otherwise resolves the conflict by leaving the RRset absent while still accepting the UPDATE.
group_1.post_state: QUERY for x.campus.edu. A returns no answer / empty rrset.

group_2.behavior: Treats the same-name add_rr and delete_rrset in one UPDATE as an invalid conflict and rejects the message, or applies a different intra-message policy.
group_2.post_state: QUERY for x.campus.edu. A remains as the original RRset, or is otherwise unchanged from pre-update state.

expected_divergence.why_divergence: RFC 2136 §3 and §3.4 define UPDATE sections and operations, but do not specify intra-message ordering or conflict resolution when one UPDATE both adds an RR to an RRset and deletes that same RRset. RFC 2181 §5.2 constrains RRset integrity, but not this update-time conflict. As a result, compliant servers may differ on whether the add is visible before the delete, whether delete wins, or whether the whole UPDATE is rejected, leading to different reply RCODES and/or post-update RRset state.
